free_text: Experimental study of the Ne-20 + C-12 system.
A 100 mug/cm**2 thick carbon target was used.
Two different systems were used for
the identification of the reaction products.
* Close to 90-degree(c.m.), the energy and angle for
both the outgoing particle and the coincident recoil
nucleus were recorded by two large-are solid state
detectors
* At forward angles, a solid-state telescope was used.
The thickness of the delta-E was 13 mum and the
solid angle was 0.2msr.The telescope energy resolution
was about 1 MeV for the Ne-20 ions detected at
25-degree(lab) This telescope was used to identify
and to measure the energy of the Ne-20 and O-16 ions
resulting from elastic or inelastic scattering and
from the four-particle transfer C-12(Ne-20,O-16)O-16
at forward angles, respectively, and to detect the C-12
recoil nuclei corresponding to elastic and inelastic
scattering at backward angles.
* Two symmetrically aligned counters at 45-degree
relative to the beam direction faced a gold target
located downstream and were used as monitors.
Dead-time corrections were taken into account.
.Optical-model and coupled-channel analysis.
SB
